Ad Text:
MARS Recruitment is partnering with a leading financial services organisation to appoint a high-calibre FP&A Manager.

This is a key role within the FP&A team, responsible for driving the planning, forecasting and performance reporting agenda, while partnering closely with senior stakeholders to support strategic decision-making.

You'll play a critical role in translating financial data into clear, actionable insights, helping shape business strategy and improve overall performance.

The Responsibilities:

Lead the end-to-end financial planning cycle (budgeting, forecasting and long-term planning)
Deliver insightful analysis on business performance, identifying key trends, risks and opportunities.
Partner with senior stakeholders across the business to support decision-making and drive commercial outcomes.
Build and maintain financial models to support scenario analysis and investment decisions
Prepare and present management reporting, including variance analysis and forward-looking insights.
Continuously improve FP&A processes, systems and reporting capability.
Act as a trusted advisor to the CFO and leadership team.
Identify risks and opportunities, providing clear recommendations to support growth and efficiency.

The Requirements:

8+ years' experience in FP&A / commercial finance.
CA / CPA / ACCA / CFA qualified.
Strong financial modelling and forecasting capability.
Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience with tools such as Power BI, Hyperion or similar.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders.
Proven ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Financial services experience preferred.

To Note:

Australian Citizenship or Permanent Residency required.

The Opportunity

Access4 is seeking an experienced Head of Product & Platform to lead the strategy, performance, and evolution of our full product portfolio, underpinned by SASBOSS®. This role is responsible for both the strategic direction and execution, developing differentiated products that meet partner needs, while advancing a scalable, secure platform that supports speed, automation, and intelligent growth.

You will inherit a live, revenue-generating portfolio spanning internally developed products, AI-enabled capabilities, and third-party integrations, and lead its next phase of expansion, verticalization, simplification, and innovation. Reporting to the COO and operating as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, this role carries end-to-end accountability for product vision, platform execution, and commercial outcomes.




What You’ll Lead

Product Portfolio Ownership

Own the end-to-end strategy and performance of Access4’s product portfolio, including our four core UC platforms, AI-enabled tools and workflows, and vendor-integrated products. Define a clear portfolio strategy across build, buy, borrow, or retire decisions, ensuring products are positioned, packaged, and priced to drive partner adoption, retention, and growth.




Platform Enablement

Own the evolution of SASBOSS® as the foundational platform that enables product velocity, automation, and partner scale. Ensure APIs and integrations are designed to be flexible and adaptable, allowing the platform to easily accommodate new features, tools, or third-party services.




AI-Native Product Innovation

Guide the introduction of AI-assisted features, products, automation, and intelligence across the product and platform portfolio. Ensure AI delivers practical, defensible value and supports data-driven product decision-making.




Go-to-Market & Commercial Impact

Ensure cross pillar collaboration with Sales and Marketing to translate product capability into clear, value-led messaging and enablement. Support pricing strategy, market and competitor testing, commercial modelling, and act as a senior voice on product value internally and to our partner network globally.




Delivery, Architecture & Trust

Lead product, platform, and engineering teams to deliver predictable, high-quality outcomes while ensuring security, resilience, and compliance expected of a business-essential SaaS platform.

Champion a culture of technical curiosity and innovation by proactively identifying, recommending and challenging emerging technologies that align with and strengthen the product and platform ecosystem. Evaluate how these technologies can be integrated to enhance scalability, performance, security, and overall partner experience.




Why This Role Is Interesting

You will lead a global product portfolio with global partners and shape how multiple products scale globally. You will lead the next evolution of our SASBOSS® platform into a cohesive and intelligent global platform experience, and operate with genuine ownership and Senior Leadership Team influence.




What You Bring

8–10+ years in senior product, platform, or SaaS leadership roles; experience scaling multi-product portfolios; strong commercial acumen; understanding of cloud platforms, APIs, and integrations; and proven people leadership, including great visionary and communication skills.




Why Access4

Access4 enables partners to automate scale through its SASBOSS® platform and product portfolio. You will join a leadership team that values ownership, Genuine conversations, trust, and building products that genuinely matter.

Role overview
We are working with a federal government client seeking a Lead Financial Analyst at EL1 level to support ICT financial management within a large digital services environment. This role focuses on budget planning, forecasting, governance and strategic financial advice.
The position is based in the ACT with hybrid working arrangements, including at least two days per week in the office.
Hourly rate is $100 to $120 per hour, depending on experience.

Key responsibilities

Develop financial frameworks to strengthen ICT budgeting and forecasting, including Opex and Capex assessment
Design and improve financial processes and governance across the ICT branch
Support the intake and financial assessment of new technology initiatives
Provide strategic and operational financial advice to senior ICT leadership
Assist Directors with budget planning, forecasting and alignment to business priorities
Develop strategies to manage financial pressures within digital services
Contribute to statutory reporting and ICT investment submissions
Deliver financial performance reporting with clear commentary
Identify opportunities to improve services to internal stakeholders
Required experience and skills

Demonstrated experience in ICT financial management within complex environments
Strong capability in budgeting, forecasting and financial analysis
Experience developing financial frameworks and governance processes
Ability to provide clear advice to senior stakeholders
Experience supporting investment decisions and policy proposals
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
Ability to link financial data to business outcomes and priorities
Additional information

Initial 12 month contract with a possible 12 month extension
Up to 40 hours per week
Baseline security clearance required, or ability to obtain
Hybrid working arrangement with flexibility subject to agreement
To discuss this opportunity confidentially, contact sarah.purdue@bluefinresources.com.au

Ad Text:
The Senior Manager, Enterprise Applications, is responsible for the strategic direction, development, and governance of QRIDA's core software assets. You will lead a high-performing team to build, test, and release digital solutions that enable QRIDA's financial services, ensuring all software changes are technically sound and successfully adopted by the business.

As the Senior Manager, Enterprise Applications, you will lead the software development team to build and maintain high-performing enterprise applications using modern architectural patterns.

Key responsibilities include:

Define the technical standards, tools, and methodologies (e.g., Agile, Scrum) used across the development lifecycle.

Establish and govern a robust organisational release strategy, including automated QA, performance, and security testing.

Oversee the release functions to ensure that all software meets defined "Definition of Done" (DoD) criteria and security benchmarks before release.

Lead the Digital Change function to manage the human impact of technology transitions, including stakeholder communication, training, and business readiness.

Manage the enterprise application budget and lifecycle, addressing technical debt and software renewals.

Work with the Senior Manager Analytics and Reporting to oversee the integration of Data Engineering and Machine Learning capabilities into user-facing applications.

For a full description of the key responsibilities, please refer to the role description.

Joining our passionate team means an opportunity to learn, further your career, have your ideas heard and make a difference for rural and regional Queenslanders every day.

Staff have access to:

Salary Packaging, 12.75% super and leave loading

Flexible working arrangements including balancing of in office/working from home arrangements

Career and Performance Development Planning with the focus on setting of goals and career development

Learning & Development including access to the QRIDA Aspire Scheme

Employee assistance and benefits programs including access to Rewards Gateway (after meeting eligibility requirements)

QRIDA is a small organisation of just over two hundred employees. We value diversity in our community and work hard to foster an environment of inclusivity and trust.

The Queensland Rural and Industry Development Authority (QRIDA) is a specialist provider of government financial and advisory support to rural and regional Queensland.

As a statutory body of the Queensland Government we have been helping primary producers and industry recover from natural disaster, improve sustainability and profitability, make a start in business, and overcome difficult conditions, for over 30 years.

As the Queensland Government's experts in rural finance, we also administer the State Government's Farm Business Debt Mediation program, the Farm Debt Reconstruction Office, conduct a biennial Rural Debt Survey, undertake research and provide advice to the Minister for Agricultural Industry Development and Fisheries and Minister for Rural Communities.

QRIDA is empowered under the Rural and Regional Adjustment Act 1994 to deliver additional programs and services across a range of industry sectors and to local, state and territory governments throughout Australia and for the Australian Government.

We're committed to do more to be a proactive, responsible and reliable provider of financial management services and assistance to create sustainable rural communities.

To see what we are working on now visit our website at www.qrida.qld.gov.au

Apply now via the below link

https://smartjobs.qld.gov.au/jobs/QLD-686278

D2i Consulting is seeking a Senior Data & Analytics Consultant / Architect to lead a high-impact Discovery & Data Strategy engagement with a leading FMCG importer and distributor supplying all major national retailers.

This is a unique opportunity to shape the future data strategy, architecture, and AI readiness of a growing, complex supply chain business operating across multiple domains including finance, operations, and sales.

You will play a critical role in defining how data can unlock smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term growth.

Key Responsibilities

You will lead a structured 4-week discovery and strategy engagement, including:

Conducting current state assessment of data landscape, architecture, and platforms

Running business workshops across key domains (Ops, Sales, Finance)

Reviewing data sources including JDE ERP, Azure, SaaS HRIS and WMS

Identifying pain points, capability gaps, and opportunity areas

Defining and prioritising high-value use cases

Designing a target-state data & analytics architecture

Developing a pragmatic 12–18 month roadmap aligned to business goals

Presenting recommendations via an Executive Strategy Pack

Engagement Scope

Discovery workshops and stakeholder alignment

Data platform and architecture review (Azure / Microsoft ecosystem)

Use case definition and prioritisation

Data strategy, governance and operating model definition

Executive-level presentation and recommendations

What We’re Looking For

We’re seeking a senior, client-facing Data & Analytics Architect with strong commercial and stakeholder skills.

Must have:

Proven experience delivering data strategy and discovery engagements

Strong expertise in modern data platforms (Azure / Microsoft stack preferred)

Experience with ERP systems (JDE or similar) and enterprise data landscapes

Ability to translate business needs into data architecture and roadmap

Excellent stakeholder engagement and workshop facilitation skills

Highly desirable:

FMCG, supply chain, or retail industry experience

Exposure to AI / advanced analytics strategy

Experience establishing data governance frameworks

Why Join This Engagement?

Shape the data strategy for a growing Australian FMCG business

Work directly with senior stakeholders and decision-makers

Deliver tangible outcomes in a short, high-impact engagement

Opportunity to extend into implementation phase

Engagement Details

Duration: ~4 weeks (Discovery phase) with potential for further work to follow with other clients

Effort: ~20 days

Start: Immediate / ASAP

Location: Coburg / Hybrid

Our client is one of Australia’s best-known organisations and is offering a genuinely interesting contract opportunity within a close-knit, supportive, and well-regarded team.

They are seeking a high-performing Power BI Specialist to join their respected Data & Analytics team on an initial 3-month contract.

This is a fast-paced, hands-on contract where you will need to hit the ground running. There is a strong pipeline of priority work already underway, so this role will suit someone who is practical, responsive, and comfortable delivering in a busy environment.

About the role

You will support the delivery of key reporting and analytics outcomes across a pipeline of critical projects. The role will require strong technical capability, strong attention to detail, and the ability to work effectively across both business and technical stakeholders.

This is a role for someone who can operate independently, contribute strongly within a team, and deliver fit-for-purpose reporting solutions in a high-pressure environment.

Key requirements

Demonstrated experience with Microsoft Power BI, SQL, and advanced Excel reporting

Proven experience building Power BI dashboards and reports using data sourced from SAP S/4HANA

Experience working with SAP S/4HANA tables and data structures to support reporting, analysis, and insight generation

Ability to extract, interpret, model, and transform S/4HANA data for use in Power BI

Experience integrating Power BI with SAP S/4HANA data sources, including supporting data flows and reporting architecture

Experience working with Microsoft Fabric to support data ingestion, transformation, modelling, and reporting for Power BI environments

Strong capability in data modelling, data flow architecture, data migration, and data transition architecture

Meticulous attention to detail and the ability to work independently

A team-focused, hands-on approach

Strong written and verbal communication skills

Ability to devise practical solutions to problems

Ability to perform under pressure and meet deadlines

About you

You are proactive, self-motivated, and comfortable working both autonomously and collaboratively. You bring a practical delivery mindset, strong technical reporting capability, and the ability to contribute immediately across a busy project pipeline.

Apply now

If you are a strong Power BI specialist with experience working across SAP S/4HANA data environments and exposure to Microsoft Fabric, we would like to hear from you.

We look forward to your application.

About the Company

This organisation is a well-established leader in the utilities sector, delivering critical services to a large and growing customer base. With a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, and future-ready technology, they are investing heavily in data, AI, and advanced analytics.




Their technology function is undergoing a major uplift, with AI positioned as a key driver of smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term value creation. You'll be joining a business where AI is not just experimental-it's becoming embedded across the enterprise.


About the Role

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.




Key responsibilities include:





Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment
Driving innovation across NLP, LLMs, and advanced ML techniques
Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.


About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.




You'll bring:





Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience

About us

LGT is the largest Private Banking and Asset Management group in the world to be owned by an entrepreneurial family. As the family office of the Princely House of Liechtenstein, we have years of experience in the management of sizeable sums of assets.




Your challenge

This is a rare opportunity to shape the future capability, leadership and skills agenda of a leading private wealth management firm at a critical stage of growth. Reporting directly to the Chief People Officer, this role offers full ownership of the learning, capability and organisational development agenda across the business. You will design and deliver an enterprise-wide strategy that builds future-ready capability, strengthens risk culture and enables long-term business success.

You will set the direction, define the priorities and lead execution end-to-end — from strategy and design through to hands-on facilitation, delivery and measurement.

This role supports a national workforce of 400+ professionals across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide and Perth and operates with genuine senior-level influence, autonomy and accountability.




Key responsibilities

Design and implement the end-to-end learning, capability and future skills strategy, aligned to business priorities, regulatory obligations and workforce transformation.

Partner with Executives and senior leaders to identify critical capability gaps and translate them into practical, high-impact development solutions.

Own the full learning lifecycle — learning needs analysis, program design, facilitation, implementation, evaluation and continuous improvement.

Build and embed career pathways, capability frameworks and development journeys that provide clarity on progression and future skills.

Design and deliver leadership and talent development programs, with a focus on people leadership, performance, coaching, feedback and change leadership.

Lead organisational development initiatives including support for talent reviews, succession planning and workforce planning.

Ensure regulatory confidence by overseeing compliance learning, Kaplan reporting, CPD frameworks and learning record integrity in partnership with Risk, Compliance and Advisory Support teams.

Use insights, data and feedback to measure impact, prioritise investment and continuously lift capability outcomes.

Leverage and partner as appropriate with our Center of Excellence L&D team in Europe




Your profile

You are a seasoned learning, capability or organisational development leader who thrives in roles requiring both strategic thinking and hands-on delivery.

You will bring

Extensive experience in Learning & Development, Capability or OD roles.

Background in financial services or similarly regulated environments (investment banking, wealth management, funds management or professional services preferred).

Proven experience building and delivering learning and capability strategy, not simply maintaining existing programs.

Strong capability in learning design and facilitation, with confidence delivering to senior and executive audiences.

Experience managing compliance and regulatory learning, including CPD frameworks and learning record systems (Kaplan experience highly regarded).

A degree in HR, Organisational Psychology, Business or a related discipline; postgraduate qualifications, coaching accreditation or psychometric tools (Hogan, Insights, ICF) are advantageous.

You are comfortable operating independently, enjoy genuine ownership and are motivated by creating meaningful, lasting impact.




Why you’ll love this role

End-to-end ownership — you set the strategy and deliver it.

Executive access and influence - shape and drive the learning agenda with senior leaders and the Executive team

Elevated remit spanning capability, leadership, talent and OD.

Recognised as one of the world’s largest IT and business consulting firms, CGI partners with clients across Australia’s public and private sectors to solve complex business challenges through technology and innovation.

We are seeking a Director Consulting Expert (DCE) – AI (Artificial Intelligence) to lead strategic transformation engagements across data modernisation, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics. This senior leadership role calls for a leader who can combine executive presence with a collaborative, inclusive approach, bringing both strategic vision and hands-on expertise to guide meaningful outcomes.

You will partner closely with clients to design and implement enterprise-grade AI and data strategies—translating business vision into data-driven outcomes that create measurable value. As a trusted advisor to senior executives, you will bring confidence, empathy, and influence, shaping conversations and building strong, lasting relationships.

A key expectation of this role is the ability to define, build, and scale AI offerings, contributing to CGI’s growth through innovative, practical, and human-centred solutions. You will also champion thought leadership, helping clients and teams navigate the evolving AI landscape with clarity and purpose.

You will enjoy flexible working, a supportive and inclusive culture, and the opportunity to contribute to a team where innovation, leadership, and growth are part of everyday work.

Your future role and responsibilities:

Lead enterprise-scale AI and data transformation programs across strategy, architecture, governance, and delivery.

Partner with C-suite stakeholders to shape AI-driven business outcomes and long-term value.

Bring a hands-on, supportive approach to solution design, architecture validation, and delivery guidance.

Demonstrate authentic executive presence, building trust and influencing strategic conversations.

Develop and implement enterprise data and AI strategies, including governance and ethical AI frameworks.

Shape and deliver AI and ML initiatives, such as predictive analytics, generative AI adoption, automation, and intelligent decision support.

Build and scale AI offerings, including reusable frameworks and industry solutions.

Contribute to business growth through client engagement, opportunity shaping, and relationship building.

Mentor and uplift teams, fostering a collaborative, inclusive, and high-performing culture.


Experience required to be successful in this role:

Proven leadership in enterprise AI and data transformation programs.

Strong understanding of data architecture, engineering, governance, and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Databricks, Snowflake, GCP).

Demonstrated hands-on experience in AI/ML solution design or implementation.

Expertise in AI technologies (machine learning, NLP, computer vision, GenAI).

Strong executive presence with the ability to influence and build trusted relationships.

Experience in developing and scaling consulting offerings and go-to-market solutions.

Excellent business development and stakeholder engagement skills.

Strong leadership, communication, and mentoring capabilities, with a focus on empowering others.


Why work for CGI?

Make a difference

From utilities and cybersecurity to government and financial service, we provide high end IT and business consulting to the world’s most vital and exciting sectors. We’re proud to partner with our clients to bring real business value and contribute to their success by delivering practical innovation that helps them meet their goals.

Your work is what you make it.

We provide a working environment where our CGI Partners are empowered to shape their careers and take on new responsibilities. As owners, we have the opportunity to drive our careers, forge meaningful relationships with our clients, and benefit from a job well done.

As owners, we’re invested in creating an environment where we enjoy working together and learning from each other. We work with teams from all across the world and celebrate our CGI Partners’ unique differences. We thrive on building strong relationships with our fellow CGI Partners and supporting each other to develop professionally and achieve our potential.

Great benefits and work-life balance

We strongly believe that work-life balance is crucial to our success and the success of our CGI Partners. At CGI, a positive work-life balance is encouraged and supported. We offer flexible work wherever possible and invest in benefits and services that are truly valued by our CGI Partners, such as our world leading health and wellbeing program, Oxygen.

Diversity statement

At CGI, we recognise the richness that diversity brings. We strive to create a work culture where everyone belongs, and we collaborate with clients in building more inclusive communities. As an equal opportunity employer, we empower all our CGI Partners to succeed and grow. 

CGI is proud to be a Work180 endorsed employer for all women.

We are committed to providing an inclusive, diverse and equal opportunity workplace. When you apply, please share any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

Recognised as one of the world’s largest IT and business consulting firms, CGI has offices across Australia, supporting local public and private sector clients to solve real business problems.

We are looking to hire a Data Modeller who will focus on translating complex business requirements into clear, scalable, conceptual, logical, and physical data models that support analytics, reporting, and operational needs. Working closely with data engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders, the Data Modeller ensures that data is well-organized, standardized, and aligned with governance and quality frameworks to enable efficient data-driven decision making across the organization.

Flexible work is available including hybrid work from client’s site at Port Melbourne  

Your future role and responsibilities:

Contribute to the design, development, and continuous refinement of conceptual, logical, and physical data models within the Data Transformation team.

Create and maintain SQL DDL scripts, along with detailed mapping and ETL documentation, to support Data Engineers in constructing and loading data models.

When required, carry out reverse engineering of existing data models from databases or SQL code to ensure alignment with current architecture and standards.

Develop and update business documentation, including process maps, taxonomies, and ontology diagrams, to provide clarity and traceability of data flows.

Maintain a strong emphasis on conceptual and business data modelling, ensuring that structures align with organisational objectives.

Interpret and translate business requirements into scalable data models that support long-term analytical and operational needs.

Assist in managing and maintaining controlled vocabularies and the corporate data catalogue to promote consistency and reuse of data assets.

Participate in modelling workshops and collaborative sessions with other Data Modellers to align on best practices and design approaches.

Adhere to existing Data Quality and Data Governance frameworks, contributing to their ongoing enhancement and ensuring compliance with modelling standards.

Build and maintain effective working relationships with subject matter experts and business stakeholders across the organisation.

Keep stakeholders and senior management informed of prioritisation decisions, project progress, and delivery timelines, managing expectations clearly and proactively.


Experience required to be successful in this role:

Sound understanding of data modelling methodologies, including Kimball, Inmon, Top-down/Bottom-up, Relational and Dimensional Modelling, Data Warehousing, and 3NF approaches.

Ability to think conceptually and apply modelling techniques such as generalisation, subtyping, and super-typing to create efficient and flexible models.

Skilled in producing Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) using a range of notations, such as Crow’s Foot and UML.

Strong technical understanding of databases, ETL/ELT pipelines, and programming languages (typically SQL), with the ability to connect these technologies to data modelling practices. (This is a hands-on role involving active work with data.)

Solid comprehension of business processes, with the ability to capture requirements accurately and translate them into effective technical designs.

Confident communicator, capable of engaging in technical discussions with both technical and non-technical audiences across all organisational levels.

Experience with cloud-based data technologies, particularly within the Microsoft Azure ecosystem, including:

Azure Data Lake

Azure Data Factory

Azure Databricks (SQL and Python)

Azure SQL Server

Azure DevOps / Git

This is a large, highly regarded institutional investment organisation with a long-standing track record of strong performance and disciplined growth. With a significant portion of assets managed internally, the business has built a sophisticated investment capability spanning listed and unlisted markets, including private equity, infrastructure, credit and real assets.

The organisation is entering a new phase of evolution, with a focus on strengthening governance, uplifting systems and data capability, and enhancing the way investment outcomes are delivered and reported. As part of this, a newly created division has been established to bring greater alignment across investment finance, reporting, and valuation governance.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a role where the foundations are strong, but there is real scope to shape, influence and build.

About the Role

You will lead a small, high-calibre team and be responsible for end-to-end oversight of investment accounting, valuation processes, financial reporting and control across a diverse portfolio. This includes listed assets as well as more complex exposures such as private markets, infrastructure and alternative investments.

Beyond BAU delivery, a key focus of the role is transformation. You will play a central role in uplifting systems, improving data integrity, strengthening controls, and driving automation across reporting processes. The environment is dynamic and evolving.

Key areas of focus include:

Oversight of investment accounting and financial reporting processes
Review and challenge of valuations, NAVs and complex accounting treatments
Strengthening of control frameworks and governance processes
Ownership of regulatory reporting inputs and compliance requirements
Leading audit engagements and managing external service providers
Driving system, data and process transformation initiatives
Coaching and developing a high-performing team

This is a highly visible role with exposure to senior stakeholders and the opportunity to influence how the function evolves.

About You

You are a technically strong and commercially minded finance leader with experience across investment reporting, fund accounting or investment operations within a complex financial services environment.

You bring a balance of hands-on capability and strategic thinking, and are comfortable navigating ambiguity while building structure and improving processes.

Your background will likely include:

CA or CPA qualification
Experience within funds management, asset ownership, or custodial environments
Strong knowledge of investment accounting, valuation and control frameworks
Exposure to complex asset classes (e.g. alternatives, private markets, derivatives)
Experience working with or managing custodians and external providers
A track record of improving processes, systems or operating models

You are:

A natural problem solver with strong attention to detail
Comfortable influencing senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams
Pragmatic, resilient and able to operate in an evolving environment
A people leader who can develop capability and lift team performance
Motivated by the opportunity to build, improve and leave something better than you found it

Job Title: Associate Director – Group Treasury (Analytics and Innovation)
Ad Text:
About us

Resimac Group is a dynamic and leading ASX-listed non-bank lender and multi-channel distribution business in Australia and New Zealand.  We enable homeowners and businesses to achieve their dreams by providing innovative, fast and accessible lending solutions to homeowners, consumers and business across Australia and New Zealand. 

Our brands include Resimac, Resimac NZ, homeloans.com.au and Resimac Asset Finance and we’re proud to be servicing over 50,000+ current customers, with assets under management of over $15 billion, and a fast-growing workforce of over 330 people operating across Australia, New Zealand and the Philippines.




Why You’ll Love This Role

This is a rare and highly visible opportunity to shape the future of Group Treasury by leading its analytics, forecasting and innovation agenda end-to-end. You will work closely with the Group Treasurer, senior executives and enterprise-wide technology teams to deliver smarter, faster and more predictive treasury insights.

You’ll gain exposure to:

End-to-end Treasury analytics across funding, capital, securitisation and trust management
Enterprise AI, data and platform transformation initiatives
Complex RMBS and ABS transactions and capital markets analysis
Strategic funding, capital and balance sheet optimisation
Senior stakeholder engagement across Finance, Risk, Technology and the Executive team

This role is ideal for a technically strong and commercially minded leader who enjoys operating at the intersection of Treasury, data and innovation.

 

The Role

As Associate Director - Group Treasury (Analytics & Innovation), you will be responsible for all aspects of Treasury data, analytics and forecasting. You will lead the design and delivery of analytical frameworks that support funding strategy, capital management, securitisation execution and enterprise decision-making.

You will also play a key role in identifying and implementing automation, digital and AI initiatives to drive efficiency, productivity and scalability across Treasury and Trust Management functions.

Key responsibilities include:

Treasury Analytics & Forecasting

Review and enhance monthly and ad‑hoc Treasury and Finance forecasting and modelling
Lead cost of funds, capital, debt and working capital forecasting
Develop and maintain product-level pricing and profitability models
Deliver portfolio risk analysis and stress testing insights

Funding, Securitisation & Capital Analytics

Provide analytical support for warehouse funding and bank facility optimisation
Lead modelling and analysis for RMBS and ABS transactions, including cashflow, WAL, credit and capital structures
Support analysis and recommendations on currency, duration and transaction structuring
Deliver investor, dealer and ratings agency analytics and reporting

Innovation, Data & Automation

Identify, design and implement data, digital and AI initiatives across Treasury and Trust Management
Participate in enterprise-wide AI and analytics initiatives
Lead implementation of new analytics platforms and tools
Review, rebuild and relaunch the debt investor portal
Identify automation opportunities to enhance reporting, controls and external disclosures

Strategic Projects & M&A

Provide valuation, return and capital impact analysis for M&A opportunities
Support enterprise strategy development relating to data, forecasting and analytics
Deliver funding and capital recommendations for growth initiatives

 

About You

You are a senior treasury or analytics professional with a strong background in financial modelling, data analytics and capital markets. You enjoy solving complex problems, challenging existing processes and using data to influence strategic outcomes.

You are comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders, confident translating complex data into clear insights, and motivated by driving continuous improvement through technology and innovation.

Skills & Experience

Extensive experience within Treasury, capital markets, securitisation or financial services
Deep expertise in financial modelling, forecasting and analytics
Strong understanding of warehouse funding, RMBS and ABS structures
Experience supporting capital, funding and balance sheet strategy
Advanced technical skills including Power BI, Tableau or similar BI tools
Advanced Excel and SQL capability
Experience delivering data, digital, automation or AI initiatives (highly valued)
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Attributes

Highly analytical with strong commercial judgement
Strategic thinker with the ability to execute and deliver outcomes
Curious and innovation-oriented mindset
Detail-focused while able to see the bigger picture
Confident presenting insights and recommendations to senior leadership
Collaborative and relationship-driven

About us

Children’s Hospital Foundation funds cutting-edge equipment, vital research and on the ground support for kids with all illnesses, injuries and conditions, throughout Queensland and northern New South Wales. We identify and action gaps in funding and support right across the kids’ health landscape – the places left lacking but no less deserving.

Be the difference

Are you ready to be part of something special? It is an exciting time to join the Children’s Hospital Foundation as we embark on a period of transformation to achieve our ambitious five-year strategy. We aren’t just evolving; we are revolutionising how we make a difference to sick kids across Queensland.

We value who you are, not just your skillset. We are looking for people who purposeful, wholehearted, collaborative, curious and courageous. People who are prepared to lead from where they stand and who are excited to build it with us.   

We are shaking things up and transforming how we operate, how we raise funds and how we deliver the greatest impact for sick and injured children and young people across Queensland. 

If this sounds like something you would like to be a part of, then we want to meet you! 

The role

The CRM Analyst plays a critical role in supporting and enhancing Children’s Hospital Foundation’s fundraising performance through effective CRM operations, reporting and data insights. Working closely with the Head of Mass Fundraising & Data Strategy and the Fundraising Operations Manager, this role ensures the stability, governance and optimisation of Dynamics 365 and related fundraising systems, while delivering meaningful analysis and reporting to inform decision‑making across the Mass Fundraising team. Acting as a key bridge between data, systems and fundraising delivery, the CRM Analyst enables data‑led supporter engagement, campaign effectiveness and revenue growth.

This is a full‑time, permanent role based at our corporate office in South Brisbane.

Who are you?

A data‑driven professional who enjoys solving complex problems and improving systems

Purpose‑led, with a genuine interest in using data to support meaningful fundraising outcomes

Confident working with CRM platforms and translating data insights for non‑technical stakeholders

Comfortable challenging systems and processes to drive continuous improvement

Organised and detail‑focused, with a strong commitment to data quality and governance

A collaborative team player who contributes positively to a supportive and inclusive culture

Key criteria

To be successful in this role, you will possess the following:

Tertiary or vocational qualification in IT, Data Analytics, or a related discipline, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education.

Microsoft certifications preferred including relevant Power Platform, Dynamics, Azure, and Power BI (other IT industry certifications considered)

3+ years’ hands‑on experience supporting and administering CRM systems, ideally Microsoft Dynamics 365

Strong data analysis capability, including writing and optimising SQL queries for reporting and insights

Demonstrated experience designing and delivering reports and dashboards using Power BI, including data modelling

Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, Microsoft Fabric, Azure Synapse or similar

Proven experience supporting system improvements, integrations and continuous optimisation initiatives

Experience administering or developing solutions within the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps)

Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate data insights into clear and actionable information for non‑technical stakeholders

Experience working with vendors and external technology partners

A collaborative approach, with the ability to work effectively across fundraising, operations and IT stakeholders

Experience in a not‑for‑profit or fundraising environment (desirable)

What is our difference?

When you join the team, you’re not just doing another ‘job’. You are playing your part, bringing about real change, creating the difference every kid deserves. Our team is passionate, inclusive, fun and caring. You will also be able to access a range of benefits and programs to help you be the best version of you, including:

A market-competitive salary package

3 bonus days of annual leave 

NFP salary packaging benefits

Work/life balance through flexible and hybrid working

Learning and development opportunities

Free, confidential counselling and wellbeing support for you and your family

Corporate health care plan

Modern open plan office overlooking South Bank

How to apply

Copy and paste the below link into your web browser, select apply for this job, complete the application form and upload your resume and a cover letter outlining your interest and suitability.

A copy of the position description can also be found through the below link. We are unable to consider your application unless you apply via this link.

https://childrenshospitalfoundation.bamboohr.com/careers/198?source=aWQ9Ng%3D%3D

Applications close COB Tuesday 5 May 2026 however, they will be reviewed during this time.

Please Note:

If you require any adjustments to submit your application, please get in touch with us at recruitment@childrens.org.au.

We welcome and actively encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, culturally and linguistically diverse people, people living with a disability, the LGBTQI community, people with neurological differences, mature age workers, as well as parents and carers returning to the workforce.

To be eligible for permanent employment within the Children’s Hospital Foundation you must be an Australian citizen or a permanent resident.

The Children’s Hospital Foundation is committed to child safety. The Working with Children (Risk Management and Screening) Act 2000 requires that people who work with children in certain categories of employment undergo the ‘working with children’ check. All Foundation employees must be eligible for a Blue Suitability Card, issued by the Department of Justice and Attorney-General. The issue and maintenance of a positive suitability notice is a requirement for continued employment at the Foundation.

Job Title: EOI - Technology & Digital Professionals - Sunshine Coast, QLD
Ad Text:
SEEKING EXPRESSIONS OF INTEREST - IT & DIGITAL PROFESSIONALS

Are you an experienced IT or Digital professional looking to secure your next opportunity across the Sunshine Coast region?

We are currently seeking Expressions of Interest from skilled professionals across a range of IT and project delivery disciplines for upcoming and anticipated opportunities across the Sunshine Coast region, QLD.

Please note: These are Expressions of Interest only. There are currently NO active roles.

These opportunities will suit candidates who are based locally or those in surrounding areas who are open to travelling to office locations across the region, including Noosaville and Maroochydore.

We are seeking a Financial Planning & Analysis Manager for a newly created role, a commercially astute leader who will set the benchmark for excellence in delivering actionable insights, driving performance improvement, and achieving strong commercial outcomes for the Group. This position offers the opportunity to make a lasting impact and define what 'good' looks like within our FP&A function with initially a very hands on approach, leading an experienced analyst to enhance our approach to business partnering and financial analysis.  

Reporting to the Deputy CFO this role will take ownership of FP&A function budgeting, forecasting and performance analysis for the Group. You're a CA or CPA qualified finance professional with significant leadership experience in FP&A with a commercial mindset. 

Key responsibilities include  

Lead and elevate the Group FP&A function across budgeting, forecasting, reporting and performance analysis 

Deliver insightful and strategic recommendations that influence business performance  

Drive cashflow forecasting/planning, working capital optimisation, and liquidity management with precision 

Own the preparation of board packs, executive reporting, and in-depth performance analysis 

Act as the key liaison for board-level financial queries and support the finance leadership in board communications. 

Lead finance system related projects and implementation programs to enhance capability and efficiency 

Strengthen governance, controls and documentation across models and reporting frameworks 

You’re a great candidate if you are 

CA or CPA qualified with strong leadership experience in FP&A 

Have a background in Retail or FMCG environments 

Hold advanced financial modelling capability, including scenario analysis, cash flow modelling and investment appraisal. 

Experience in Leading the implementation of ERP/major financial system projects  

A sharp analytical mindset, attention to detail and confidence working with large complex data sets 

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills  

What’s on offer 

Direct exposure to senior finance leaders and the Board 

Flexible working arrangements 

Ongoing learning and development opportunities 

Generous team member discount 

Paid parental leave 

Employee Assistance Program 

Discounted health insurance 


Apply today if this role is for you.

A2B Australia is part of the ComfortDelGro Australia (CDC) Group, the country’s leading taxi network and a major provider of mobility, technology, and payment solutions.

Through our iconic brands 13cabs, Silver Service, Swan Taxis and Cabcharge we deliver end‑to‑end transport experiences, from booking and dispatch through to safe, reliable travel and seamless payment. Our services support corporate, community and disability customers nationwide.
Our network is Australia’s largest, with 7,000+ vehicles and 11,500 drivers, supported by contact centres, technology hubs and in‑field support teams. We power drivers, operators and passengers with dispatch, navigation, in‑car tech, apps and payment solutions across Australia and globally.
  
The A2B Way
We care about our customers, our business and each other. We work as one connected team, value accountability and collaboration, and encourage innovation to shape the future of mobility. We invest in wellbeing, safety, learning and development, recognise high performance, and celebrate diversity and inclusion.

The Role

We’re looking for a talented Insight Engineer to join our growing data team within a fast‑paced taxi and payments business. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI, playing a critical part in turning complex data into trusted, actionable insights that support smarter business decisions.

Working closely with business stakeholders, analysts, and data scientists, you’ll design and deliver scalable data pipelines, translate business needs into robust data models, and enable advanced analytics and AI use cases. This is a hands‑on, high‑impact role where your work will directly influence how the business operates, grows, and innovates. 

What you’ll be doing

Design, build, and maintain scalable Azure data pipelines and platforms across Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Lake
Develop and optimise data models supporting data warehouse, big data, and Data Vault architectures
Deliver reliable batch and streaming data solutions, ensuring high data quality and performance
Translate business requirements into curated datasets and semantic layers that power reporting, analytics, and decision‑making
Partner closely with stakeholders to generate actionable insights from complex datasets such as payments, trips, and customer behaviour
Implement CI/CD and infrastructure‑as‑code practices to improve reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency
Support emerging AI and advanced analytics use cases, working with data scientists on feature engineering and model deployment (where applicable).
About you
 
We’re looking for an experienced Insight Engineer who has operated within enterprise‑scale organisations, managing complex data environments and working across multiple stakeholders. Our ideal candidate brings experience in data or analytics engineering, with strong hands‑on expertise across the Azure data stack and a solid grounding in data warehousing, data modelling, and ETL/ELT best practices. You’re comfortable working with high‑volume, business‑critical data, translating insight requirements into robust technical solutions, and partnering closely with the business to deliver reliable, scalable outcomes.

Skills and experience

Required 

5–7+ years experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or insight engineering roles  
Strong hands-on experience with Azure data stack:  
Azure Data Factory  
Azure Synapse Analytics  
Azure Data Lake
Solid understanding of:  
Data warehousing concepts (star schema, dimensional modelling)  
Big data processing frameworks  
Data Vault modelling 
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Stored procedure, PowerShell,  
Experience with real-time/streaming data (e.g., Kafka, Event Hubs)  
At least one programming language (Python preferred)  
Experience building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines  
Strong understanding of business data and ability to translate insight requirements into technical solutions  
Ready to apply?

If you’re an experienced Insight Engineer looking to work in a complex, enterprise environment and make a meaningful impact through data, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

ComfortDelGro Corporation Australia (CDC) and A2B acknowledges the Traditional Custodians throughout Australia, and their continuing connection to land, waters and community. We pay our respects to all First Nations people, their culture and to Elders past and present.

Product Analyst - Optimisation Team Lead
Sydney office based, Mitchell Street, McMahons Point, NSW, 2060 
Team leader position 
Hybrid working model (2 days working from home & 3 days working in the office)

 

Compass Group is listed on the London Stock Exchange (Top 20) and is the global market leader in providing food and a range of support services to customers in the workplace, schools and colleges, hospitals, at leisure and in remote environments. We operate in over 50 countries, employ over 600,000 people, and are a recognized top 10 employer worldwide. In Australia, Compass Group Australia (CGA) generates $2.4Bn revenue per annum, servicing over 350 clients across over 700 sites, with 15,000 employees.

Join Compass Group and play a key role in shaping high-quality, sustainable food solutions across our diverse client portfolios. In this role, you will lead structured SKU and menu optimisation initiatives, combining culinary expertise with data-driven insight to deliver measurable value for our clients. Working closely with culinary, procurement, and sector stakeholders, you will translate complex analysis into practical recommendations that support smarter decision-making and long-term outcomes.
 
About the Role

As Product Analyst - Optimisation Team Lead, you will be a key team member of Central Culinary' team, plus a critical part in delivering incremental financial and non-financial benefits to member clients through data-driven analysis, culinary insight, and strong stakeholder engagement.

You will act as the key conduit between Procurement, Culinary teams, and Compass Group Australia sectors, supporting menu development, tender responses, and optimisation initiatives while leading a small national culinary services team.

Key Responsibilities
Perform net pricing, scenario, leakage, and inflation analysis. 
Lead end-to-end SKU optimisation programs, from opportunity identification through to consultation, approval, and delivery
Deliver structured reporting on optimisation performance, benefits realised, and initiatives in progress
Provide culinary and commercial expertise to support product rationalisation, optimisation, and alternate recommendations
Partner with procurement teams on optimised product specifications for tenders and sourcing activities
Work closely with sector culinary teams to develop and validate menus
Manage national stakeholder relationships with Sector GMs and Executive Directors
Lead and develop a team of culinary services professionals
Support business development activity with commercial data, modelling, and supplier collateral

 

What You’ll Bring

Essential:

Strong commercial and analytical capability with experience working with complex data
Sound understanding of pricing, costing, and commercial principles
Advanced Excel skills (financial modelling, pivots, lookups)
Clear, professional communication skills and strong stakeholder engagement capability
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to timeframes

Desirable:

Experience in procurement, sourcing, contracting, and culinary environments
Exposure to FMCG, food, catering, or hospitality industries
Experience with tools such as Tableau, SQL, or Access
Relevant tertiary qualification (STEM, Finance, Economics, Supply Chain or similar)

 

Ready to make a meaningful impact while advancing your career? Apply now and be part of something bigger. You’ll collaborate with culinary and cross-functional stakeholders to interpret insights, share outcomes clearly, and support smarter, faster decision-making across product and menu optimisation. Join Compass Group’s Culinary team and help deliver sustainable, client-focused food solutions.

 

Working with a large global company means you can be assured that we have an attractive benefits program! This includes comprehensive learning & development offerings, a variety of recognition programs and company discounts, an excellent salary benchmarking matrix and at this management level also includes an incentive bonus scheme! You can read all about these here: Why work at Compass

We are committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you require adjustments or alternative methods of communication in the recruitment process, please reach out to us on either talisson@compass-group.com.au or georgefouskarinis@compass-group.com.au

Initial 6-month contract + extensions
Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification required
Location: CBD- Hybrid work model
Our client is looking for a Senior Process Analyst to join a large AI transformation initiative.

Working closely with business stakeholders, you will carry out 'As Is' analysis and identify how to optimise the process and implement a solution to uplift efficiency.  You will be involved in the design of the future target state, as well as the optimisation and uplift of productivity. You'll be supporting business outcomes, around how to reduce leakage and improve outcomes in the process.

Key Responsibilities:

Conduct ‘As Is analysis
Implement a business process management framework to systematically document processes including process mapping, defining key performance indicators and controls and identifying risks.
Essential skills and experience

Previous experience as a Process Analyst
Strong business process transformation experience
Must have Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification (mandatory).
Experience delivering large-scale, enterprise-wide transformation
Ability to lead workshops and facilitate senior stakeholders
Work strategically (future-state design) AND hands-on (process mapping)
Experience within financial services is highly desirable
For more info contact Aparna on Aparna.sharma@peoplebank.com.au
 


Peoplebank and Leaders IT are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone belongs. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need adjustments to the recruitment process due to your circumstances, please let us know—we’re here to support you.

This is a rare opportunity for a proven executive leader to step into a high-impact role driving large-scale programs across government, industry and research.
 
About This Role
This is a rare opportunity to step into a critical executive role with a national mandate, leading a portfolio that sits at the centre of government engagement, industry engagement across the energy and VET sectors, workforce strategy, and research.
The organisation is entering its next phase of maturity, and this role is pivotal in shaping how strategy is translated into coordinated, system-wide delivery across the entire portfolio.
You will take over a well-established function and be expected to build on strong foundations, bringing credibility, authority and a clear strategic voice to both internal and external stakeholders.
This is not just a relationship or advisory role - success will come from someone who can think at a systems level, communicate a clear vision, and demonstrate how they have personally led and delivered complex, national programs with measurable impact.
 
Why This Role Matters
This portfolio plays a central role in:

Influencing government policy and strengthening national relationships
Driving workforce planning, strategy and sector-wide insights
Integrating research, data and industry engagement into actionable outcomes
Lifting organisational capability and aligning delivery across a national footprint
Leading tripartite engagement across government, industry and unions to drive aligned outcomes
 As part of the executive team, you will help shape the organisation’s strategic direction, credibility and long-term impact.
 

Key Benefits

National executive role with direct influence on policy, workforce strategy and sector outcomes
Opportunity to lead a critical, high-visibility portfolio at the centre of government, industry and research
Work closely with the CEO, Executive and Board, contributing to enterprise-wide strategy and direction
Lead and develop a high-calibre, multi-disciplinary team, shaping capability at scale
Drive large-scale, nationally significant programs with measurable economic and workforce impact
Play a key role in the organisation’s next phase of maturity, structure and influence
 
Key Responsibilities

Lead a national portfolio, aligning government, energy and VET industry engagement, research and workforce initiatives
Drive strategic government engagement, strengthening policy influence and key relationships
Lead industry and union engagement, operating effectively within a tripartite model
Provide clear thought leadership, setting direction and articulating how outcomes will be delivered
Lead and uplift a senior leadership team, building capability and accountability across the function
Translate systems thinking into execution, connecting strategy to measurable results
Oversee workforce insights and research, ensuring outputs are rigorous, relevant and designed to influence policy and industry outcomes
Strengthen internal capability, ensuring consistent delivery across the full portfolio
Contribute to financial strategy and decision-making, linking investment to impact
Deliver high-quality submissions, briefings and strategic outputs for executive and Board audiences
 
What You’ll Bring

Proven experience leading enterprise-level, national programs or portfolios of significant scale and complexity
Background in federal/state government, national NFPs, universities or industry bodies delivering sector-wide impact
Demonstrated ability to operate as a systems leader, translating strategy into coordinated, multi-stream delivery
Highly developed executive communication skills, able to articulate vision, direction and outcomes with clarity and authority
Clear track record of owning and solving complex problems end-to-end, with tangible outcomes
Credibility and presence to influence senior stakeholders, including government, industry and union representatives
Strong financial and commercial acumen, with confidence contributing to investment and funding decisions
Experience leading senior, multi-disciplinary teams, with the ability to challenge, influence and uplift experienced leaders
A forward-thinking, strategic mindset, comfortable operating in ambiguity and driving change
 
This is a genuinely unique role suited to a proven executive leader already operating at, or ready to step into, General Manager level, with the ability to bring authority, structure and strategic clarity to a critical function.
 
If you think this could be the role for you then please apply with your CV and Cover Letter.

Alternatively, if you know someone looking - refer them to us and we’ll give you $500 if we find them a new role.

AI Governance Specialist

Sydney, NSW | Contract

The Opportunity

A leading NSW government organisation is seeking an experienced AI Governance Specialist to lead the development and implementation of enterprise-wide AI governance practices. This is a high-impact role focused on ensuring the safe, ethical, and compliant use of Artificial Intelligence across a large, complex environment.

You will play a critical role in shaping governance frameworks, embedding risk controls, and driving organisational adoption of AI policies-working closely with Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement, and ICT teams.

This role is advisory and assurance-focused, influencing outcomes across the business. It does not involve building or delivering AI solutions.

Key Responsibilities
Develop and maintain AI governance frameworks, policies, and standards aligned to NSW and global best practices
Lead the AI risk management process, including system classification, risk assessments, and treatment oversight
Embed AI governance into procurement and contract management, including supplier obligations and evaluation criteria
Partner with Legal, Privacy, Ethics, and Procurement teams to develop AI-specific contractual clauses and guidance
Coordinate ethical and privacy reviews, ensuring outcomes are captured in governance artefacts
Establish and manage the organisation's AI system register and assurance processes
Monitor evolving AI regulations and standards, advising on impacts and required changes
Provide reporting, insights, and assurance to senior executives and governance forums
Lead AI change and adoption strategies, including training, communications, and capability uplift
Develop practical resources such as playbooks, FAQs, and micro-learning content
Deliver training on AI safety, ethics, privacy-by-design, and responsible use
Track and report on AI adoption, compliance, and governance maturity metrics
Act as a central point of contact for AI governance queries and escalation pathways
Build and maintain a network of AI champions across the organisation
Key Relationships

Internal:

Chief Data Officer & Data Governance Office
Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement & ICT teams
Project Managers & Product Owners

External:

NSW Government stakeholders and assurance bodies
Vendors and suppliers (governance & compliance oversight)
About You

You are a senior governance professional with deep expertise in AI, data governance, and risk frameworks. You're comfortable operating in complex environments and influencing stakeholders at all levels.

Key Requirements:

10+ years' experience in Data and/or AI Governance
Proven experience implementing governance tools such as Collibra, IBM Watson, Credo AI, Informatica, or similar
Strong background in policy development, risk management, and procurement governance
Hands-on experience with AI risk management, including system classification and risk assessment frameworks
Strong knowledge of frameworks such as NSW AIAF, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 23894
Experience embedding ethical AI practices (bias mitigation, transparency, fairness)
Demonstrated ability to partner across Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement, and Data teams
Excellent stakeholder engagement, communication, and influencing skills
Why Apply?
Shape AI governance at scale within a large, complex organisation
Work at the forefront of AI policy, ethics, and regulation
Influence executive decision-making and compliance outcomes
Drive meaningful change in how AI is safely adopted

Program Change Analyst – Claims Transformation Program

6-Month Contract | Brisbane (Hybrid) | Large-Scale Transformation Program

Role Overview

We are seeking a capable Change Analyst to join a major enterprise-wide transformation program within a well-established Queensland organisation.

This role will operate at the program level, supporting multiple workstreams and providing critical visibility across change activities. It is a highly analytical and coordination-focused role, suited to someone who enjoys working with data, stakeholders, and structured artefacts to drive informed decision-making.

While aligned to change management, this role is less focused on end-to-end change delivery and more centred on analysis, reporting, and program coordination.

Key Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and analyse program-level stakeholder registers (including SMEs and key stakeholders)

Consolidate inputs from multiple initiatives to provide centralised visibility of change impacts and activities

Support the development and management of communication plans, calendars, and intranet content

Conduct stakeholder and engagement analysis, providing insights and recommendations to support effective communication strategies

Assist with program reporting and governance artefacts

Support workshop coordination and logistics, particularly as the program moves into design phases

Work closely with the Change Lead to ensure alignment and consistency across program-level change activities

What We’re Looking For

Proven experience as a Change Analyst, Change Coordinator, or Project Coordinator within complex environments

Strong analytical capability, with the ability to interpret data and provide meaningful insights

Advanced skills in Excel and PowerPoint (essential)

Experience supporting large-scale transformation or program environments

Ability to work proactively and autonomously, with a strong attention to detail

Excellent organisational and coordination skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities

Strong communication skills, with the ability to support stakeholder engagement activities

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing:

The Finance Manager, Group Financial Planning & Analysis role is to be responsible for supporting the FP&A leadership team to deliver on the strategic and financial goals of the group. This will be delivered by aligning the finance function with business activity, underpinning budgets and forecasts, through to the impact of cash flow, and the variable and fixed components of the cost base over the cycle. The role involves reporting and providing insights on a regular basis to identify forecast risks and performance.

This role is a 14 month, maximum term contract (you will still have access to annual leave, sick leave and our wonderful travel benefits). The position reports into the Head of Financial Planning & Analysis and this role has 2 direct reports.

Further responsibilities include:

Oversee the preparation and presentation of the group management accounts for Board, executive member and their leadership team discussions including supporting the executive in reviewing the financial performance of their leadership team’s departments
Oversee the preparation and provision of group quarterly reforecasts and any recurring structured budget development, ensuring accurate and appropriate use of key business performance metrics and relevant function owner support
Ownership of the Group’s Capital Planning Process, including management of the governance process and benefits tracking
Ownership and oversight as the senior business partner for the Corporate executives including Finance, Legal, Risk, People, Strategy and Corporate Affairs & Sustainability.
Support the Group Reporting Team for statutory reporting including, Impairment paper, Going Concern and CFO supporting notes
Provision of ACCC, Financier and Shareholder Quarterly reporting
Develop effective and influencing working relationships across all levels of management and with the wider FP&A team
Build and validate complex business cases in connection with business transformation, ensuring to justify potential investment and quantifying benefit, in support of the company’s growth strategy
Leadership of the Financial Planning and Analysis teams both your direct reports and the wider team
Assess and implement the most appropriate team structure, ensuring team leaders have the right leadership skills and subject matter capability for their respective functions
Build and maintain effective relationships within and across the business units & departments
Proactively work with internal stakeholders across the VA Group and external stakeholders, as required

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

CA/ CPA or equivalent qualifications
Tertiary qualifications in Business/ Economics/Commercial
Extensive experience in business partnering and finance support of business units or the wider Finance team
Experience within a large, publicly listed, corporate business
Extensive leadership experience
Extensive experience in developing forecast models and complex business cases
Development of KPIs and performance huddles for new business proposals

Company Description

Job Description


About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Analyst who excels across the disciplines of data, strategy and execution.

Reporting into the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ), this role is pivotal in connecting data-driven insights with commercial decision-making across the ANZ Go-to-Market (GTM) function. You will translate complex analysis into clear recommendations that shape strategy, enable operational efficiency and support execution at scale.

Working cross-functionally with key ANZ stakeholders — including Sales and Customer Success leadership — you will develop a deep understanding of the strategic, operational and financial drivers of the Australian business, research and analyse emerging trends and performance outcomes, identify improvement opportunities, and take a hands-on approach to validating and implementing solutions.

The focus of the role is to strengthen the analytical foundation of the ANZ GTM function, improving visibility of revenue performance drivers, enhancing performance reporting, and where applicable, supporting the wider Global Commercial Finance and RevOps teams in refining GTM systems and processes. You will play a critical role in shaping the operational landscape, supporting month-end insights, and ensuring our ANZ GTM teams are empowered with timely, accurate and actionable data.

What You'll Do

Revenue Reporting, Forecasting and Analysis

Attend and contribute to weekly ANZ forecast calls with Sales and Customer Success leaders, interpreting and sense-checking pipeline and revenue numbers.
Contribute to the weekly Flash Report and other regular reporting cycles.
Understand and interpret key ANZ metrics including new bookings, churn, downgrade, retention and price increase performance.
Together with the data team, design and maintain dashboards and visual reporting templates to surface financial performance trends, patterns and insights to support ANZ GTM leadership decision-making.
Partner with senior management to enable data-driven decisions, identifying data requirements across market analysis, planning, monitoring, financial modelling, pricing and reporting.
Support the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ) by contributing to budgeting, forecasting and reporting for consolidation.

Commission & Month-End Support

Provide ANZ-specific context and input into commission calculations and month-end processes, partnering with the global Commercial Finance team to ensure accuracy and local relevance.
Interpret ANZ-specific commission structures and edge cases, flagging issues and opportunities as they arise.

Strategic Analysis

Deliver analysis and insights on key strategic issues on an ad-hoc basis, including market intelligence, competitive landscape, product and pricing optimisation and growth opportunities.
Partner with GTM, Product and Finance teams to support strategic planning, investment decisions and commercial growth initiatives.
Build business cases and scenario models to assess new markets, partnerships and GTM strategies.

Stakeholder Engagement & Reporting

Own key stakeholder relationships with ANZ GTM leadership, supporting business case development and KPI reporting.
Deliver insights and recommendations that inform strategic and operational decision-making across the ANZ GTM organisation.

Process Evaluation & Improvement

Working alongside the RevOps team, assess, evaluate, and optimise business processes, anticipating requirements, identifying gaps and developing solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy and scalability.

Global Collaboration

Build and maintain strong relationships with global business partners, ensuring consistent understanding of data flows, inputs and outputs from multiple systems.
Act as a bridge between ANZ and global teams to standardise reporting and analytical processes.
Mentor and coach junior staff members within the team where applicable.

Qualifications


Experience

5+ years' experience in commercial finance, business analytics or commercial operations — ideally within a SaaS or technology business.
Go-to-market or commercial finance experience is highly regarded — familiarity with pipeline, renewals, commissions, bookings and churn metrics is a strong advantage.

Skills

Strong commercial acumen with deep analytical skills.
Lateral and critical thinking; ability to self-start and execute on non-process driven outcomes.
Advanced Excel skills with the ability to build and manage complex financial models.
Proven ability to influence decision-makers and present data-driven insights with clarity and impact.
Experience in revenue forecasting, financial analysis, and a solid understanding of KPIs and commercial drivers.
Hands-on experience with BI, data warehousing, statistical or reporting tools.
Experience with FP&A tools such as Adaptive, TM1 or Dynamics preferred.
Working knowledge of CRM systems; Salesforce experience is advantageous.
Familiarity with SQL, Tableau or similar analytics and visualisation platforms preferred.

Personal Attributes

Hands-on self-starter with strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills.
Comfortable operating outside of standardised procedures in a fast-moving environment.
Growth-oriented mindset with a drive to continuously improve and adapt.
Able to read a room — knows when something needs to be escalated and acts on it.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present complex financial topics clearly to non-finance stakeholders.
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.

Education

Relevant tertiary qualifications in business or a related field.
Financial qualification favourable but not essential.

Additional Information


Working at Nearmap
We move fast, we care about craft, and we're proud of what we're building. If you're energised by turning hard problems into real-world impact, we'd love to meet you

Watch our culture video below to find out more about what a day in the life at Nearmap looks like.

Nearmap does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies and search firms. Please do not email or send unsolicited resumes to any Nearmap employee, location or address. Nearmap is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.
