We’re seeking an experienced Business Analyst with strong SAP SuccessFactors and/or SAP HCM data experience to support a major transformation programme.

This role will focus on position and workforce data, ensuring data quality, supporting migration activities, reconciliation, reporting, audit support, and maintaining data integrity across systems.

Key responsibilities
Gather and document business and data requirements
Support data migration, mapping and validation activities
Perform data reconciliation across multiple systems
Analyse position and workforce data for quality and integrity issues
Support reporting, audit requests and data governance activities
Work closely with business, functional and technical stakeholders across the programme
Assist with testing, defect resolution and data-related process improvement
About you
Proven experience as a Business Analyst in SAP data-focused roles
Strong experience with SAP SuccessFactors and/or SAP HCM
Experience in data migration, data mapping, reconciliation and data quality
Strong understanding of workforce and position data structures
Experience working in complex transformation or implementation programmes
Strong stakeholder engagement and documentation skills

This is an excellent opportunity to join a high-profile programme and contribute to a critical data workstream.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

*12 Month FTC*

SEEK's growth ambition extends beyond core job ads. The Adjacent Revenue Development (ARD) team validates and accelerates the growth strategies that turn SEEK's acquired and adjacent businesses into scalable, repeatable revenue, before handing off to SEEK core teams to continue to grow.

The New Product Commercialisation Manager – Adjacent Initiatives leads the commercial acceleration of Sidekicker, SEEK's flexible staffing marketplace, as it moves from Validation phase into Product Market Fit in FY27. You will own three interconnected growth plays: building a demand intelligence engine that surfaces Sidekicker at the moment of flex labour need; designing and scaling an outbound sales and marketing motion that improves CAC and ARPU against cold lead benchmarks; and scaling a SEEK Account Manager referral program that activates SEEK's deep hirer relationships as qualified pipeline.

You will report to the Head of Growth – Adjacent Initiatives, lead a squad of Growth Operations, Analytics and Growth Executives, and work cross-functionally across SEEK's Product, Marketing and Sales teams and Sidekicker's existing Growth team.
 

What You'll Be Accountable For

Validating and executing the acceleration strategy - Identify key commercial uncertainties, design experiments to resolve them quickly, and translate results into scalable growth playbooks, accountable for FY27 OKRs
Building the demand intelligence engine - Lead the discovery, design and implementation of a lead scoring model that turns data ‘signals’ into a qualified pipeline to execute against, and develop winning GTM playbooks to scale
Designing and scaling the SEEK Account Manager referral program - Design, enable and scale a referral program that qualifies SEEK hirers against Sidekicker's ICP, building a commercial motion replicable across future adjacent initiatives.
Leading your squad with commercial rigour - Direct Growth Operations, Analytics and Growth Executives to run disciplined experiments, with hypotheses, metrics and guardrails defined before you start and outcomes turned into repeatable plays.
Cross-functional orchestration - You are the threading leader that keeps SEEK Commercial, Product, and Marketing as well as Sidekicker's own teams aligned on priorities and progress, navigating the tension between moving fast in validation and building something SEEK core teams can eventually scale.
Qualifications

Essential qualifications, skills and experience

Significant experience personally owning revenue targets and delivering measurable commercial growth outcomes, you can point to specific results and explain exactly how you drove them
Extensive hands-on experience building and executing go-to-market models for products moving from validation to scale in tech, SaaS, management consulting, or a startup or new venture environment
Significant experience designing and implementing signals-based lead scoring or targeting models that turned data into a qualified, actionable pipeline
Extensive experience leading disciplined commercial experiments, defining hypotheses, success metrics and guardrails upfront, and translating results into repeatable growth playbooks
Demonstrated ability to build alignment and drive outcomes across complex, matrix or cross-functional environments where you held significant accountability without direct authority
Extensive experience presenting growth strategies and commercial recommendations to senior leadership, and facilitating structured planning sessions across diverse, cross-functional teams
Proven experience leading or managing a squad or team, directing analytical and operational resources to execute against commercial goals
Active, applied use of AI tools to accelerate growth analysis, surface hypotheses or scale experimentation

Do these behaviours/mindset expectations resonate with your skills & experience?

Commercially driven – You connect strategy to revenue outcomes, own your numbers and can point to the specific growth results you personally delivered and exactly how you drove them
Experiments-first thinker – You don't wait for perfect information, you identify the key uncertainty, design a fast experiment to resolve it and act decisively on what you learn
Analytically sharp – Equally at home in a dataset and in a room with an executive, you turn complex analysis into crisp, compelling recommendations that earn a green light
Low-ego collaborator – You build alignment through trust and sharp thinking in environments where almost everyone you need to move doesn't report to you
Clear and precise communicator – You hold a room, write recommendations that earn decisions and turn ambiguous briefs into clear plans others can execute against
Cross-functional orchestrator – You keep commercial, product and marketing teams aligned on priorities and progress, navigating the tension between moving fast and building something scalable
AI-curious and applied – You actively explore AI tools as a multiplier for growth, insight and execution — not as a shortcut, but paired with strong commercial judgement
Comfortable with ambiguity – You thrive where the playbook doesn't yet exist and are energised by the challenge of building it
Accountability mindset – You're accountable for outcomes you don't directly control and comfortable with that responsibility
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
SEEK is committed to operating sustainably and is preparing for the impacts of climate change and the transition to a low-carbon future, and is working to minimise its environmental impacts which includes a long-term emissions reduction target of net zero by FY2050
Access to a wide range of discounts on things such as health insurance, fitness, food, travel, accommodation plus many more
Frequent events including sports days, annual Christmas party, hackathon, and trivia
Free kick-start breakfast every morning and fresh fruit available all day in our offices
Casual dress – every day

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Ad Text:
Graph Engineer

Location: Sydney CBD, 3 days per week on-site (Hybrid)

About the Role:
We are seeking a skilled engineer to support the development of a connected data platform that enables advanced analytics and intelligent automation across the business. This role focuses on building scalable data solutions that capture relationships between datasets and make them accessible for downstream use. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams to design, implement, and optimise data services that power key business initiatives.

About the Client:
Our client is a leading organisation investing heavily in modern data capabilities to drive innovation and efficiency. They foster a collaborative environment with a strong focus on technology-led transformation.

Key Responsibilities:
Design and build scalable data platforms that support relationship-driven datasets
Develop and maintain data ingestion pipelines integrating multiple structured and unstructured sources
Implement efficient query layers and APIs to enable flexible data access and analytics
Optimise data storage, indexing, and performance across large-scale datasets
Collaborate with architects and domain experts to align data models with business needs
Contribute to best practices around data modelling, governance, and platform design
Required Skills / Experience:
3-5+ years' experience in data engineering, backend engineering, or similar roles
Strong experience working with complex data models and non-relational data systems
Proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing and integration
Experience building and optimising data pipelines across multiple sources
Familiarity with modern DevOps practices and tools (e.g. Git, CI/CD pipelines, Terraform)
Ability to design performant data access patterns and query solutions
Nice to Have:
Exposure to graph or semantic data technologies
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Understanding of data modelling techniques for highly connected datasets
Experience supporting analytics, AI, or machine learning use cases


This is a great opportunity to work on cutting-edge data platforms where understanding and leveraging relationships within data is key to delivering business value.

Senior Project Manager - Examination / Accreditation Uplift

12 Month Fixed Term Contract

$180k package (base + super) + $15k salary packaging
Melbourne | Hybrid Working

About the Organisation

Talenza is partnering with a highly regarded medical organisation undertaking an enterprise-wide transformation, with an accreditation system refresh forming a key pillar of this program.

This organisation plays a central role in maintaining professional standards nationally. The program represents an investment in digital capability, governance uplift and operational effectiveness.


This is a business-led transformation with strong executive and board visibility.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Senior Project Manager to lead the end-to-end refresh of the organisation's accreditation / examination process.

You will work closely with Digital and Technology teams but remain firmly business-facing - ensuring the selected solution aligns with strategic objectives, regulatory requirements and stakeholder expectations.

This role requires a commercially astute operator who can confidently drive:

Business case development and approval
Structured vendor evaluation and selection
Executive-level stakeholder engagement
Governance and steering committee reporting



Key Responsibilities

Lead the full project lifecycle from concept through to delivery readiness
Develop and secure approval for detailed business cases
Drive RFP/RFT processes including vendor shortlisting and evaluation
Manage complex stakeholder groups including senior medical professionals and academics
Establish governance frameworks, reporting cadence and risk management processes
Ensure alignment between business, digital and external vendors

Key Selection Criteria

Demonstrated experience preparing and gaining approval for complex business cases
Experience working within academia, health, medical or clinical settings - highly regarded
Proven track record delivering business-led digital transformation programs
Strong experience running vendor evaluation and procurement processes
Ability to operate within regulated or services environments
Excellent executive communication and stakeholder management skills

Benefits

12-month Fixed Term engagement
Maximum salary package - $180k package (base + super) + $15k salary packaging - $195k package
High-impact program with senior visibility
Hybrid working environment

Are you are looking for a role that moves you closer to commercial decision-making? Rather than being confined to month-end reporting or retrospective analysis, you will be in a position to influence how the business grows, invests and improves performance across APAC. This is the kind of opportunity that gives you meaningful exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to build further depth in pricing, promotions, customer profitability, forecasting and strategic investment analysis.

You will be joining a business where finance is expected to add commercial value. That means your work will be used to shape decisions, challenge assumptions and support better outcomes across Sales, Marketing and Category. If you enjoy working in roles where your analysis has real traction and where you can see a clear link between your input and business results, this should stand out.

There is also genuine breadth in the role. You will have the chance to strengthen your experience across business partnering, modelling, planning and commercial performance analysis, while also contributing to areas such as new product activity, channel strategy, investment proposals and S&OP. This role would suit someone wanting to round out their commercial finance profile and build a stronger long-term CV. It offers the sort of variety and visibility that helps position you for broader leadership opportunities over time.

The environment will suit someone who likes pace, complexity and being part of conversations that matter. You will work with stakeholders across the business, helping bring financial clarity to decisions in a fast-moving consumer setting. There is enough structure to provide stability, but enough scope for you to make the role your own and add value beyond the obvious.

You will likely bring experience in commercial finance, FP&A or financial decision support, ideally from FMCG or manufacturing, along with strong modelling capability, advanced Excel and exposure to ERP and BI tools. CA or CPA is expected. Beyond that, the real fit will be someone who wants to step into a role where commercial judgement matters and where finance has a seat at the table.

If you are ready for a role that will stretch your commercial capability, broaden your stakeholder exposure and give you more influence over business performance, this is well worth a closer look. Apply now for a confidential discussion.

In this high-impact role, you won't just be following rules-you'll be building the blueprint for how a complex, large-scale organization adopts AI. You will bridge the gap between cutting-edge innovation and robust integrity, ensuring that as we move faster, we move safely.

As our Ai governance lead you'll develop and maintain the policies, risk controls and change strategies that define our AI journey.You will act as the master orchestrator between Legal, Privacy, Ethics, and Procurement, ensuring every AI initiative is backed by rigorous assurance and transparency.

Empower the organisation:Create the "Playbooks" and training programs that translate complex regulations into practical, everyday guidance for our teams.
Strategic advisory:Provide expert evidence and insights to senior executives and external regulators.
Build a movement:Establish a network of AI Champions to reinforce a culture of responsible and effective AI use across the agency.

Your experience:


Experience: 10+ years in Data and /or AI Governance
Hands on experience with Collibra, IBM Watson, Credo AI, or Informatica.
Deep knowledge of AI frameworks (eg: NSW AIAF) and global AI regulations
Excellent stakeholder management with a proven ability to partner with diverse functions
Strong ability to design adoption strategies that stick

At Randstad Digital, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

The Company
This leading retail organisation is recognised as a market leader within the Australian retail sector, with a strong focus on digital growth, customer experience, and omnichannel capability. With continued investment across its digital and eCommerce platforms, the business is driving a large scale uplift across online and mobile channels.

The Role
This is a 6 month day rate contract for a mid to senior Business Analyst to join a high performing digital delivery environment within the retail sector. You will play a key role across digital and eCommerce initiatives, working across web platforms and supporting mobile app related workstreams, with a strong focus on improving customer journeys and digital experience across the retail domain.

Responsibilities
* Lead end to end Business Analysis across digital and eCommerce platforms within a retail environment
* Work closely with product, UX, and engineering teams to define and deliver digital solutions
* Elicit and document requirements across customer journey, online experience, and retail digital touchpoints
* Support delivery across web platforms and contribute to mobile app initiatives where relevant
* Operate within Agile delivery teams, contributing to sprint planning, backlog refinement, and stakeholder engagement

Candidate Experience
* Proven experience as a Business Analyst within the retail sector, ideally across digital and eCommerce environments
* Strong exposure to digital platforms, customer journey mapping, and online retail experience
* Experience working across web technologies with any exposure to mobile app environments highly regarded

Please note, we can only contact successfully shortlisted candidates. This process typically takes 3 to 5 business days.

Ad Text:
Principal Data Analyst

Contract for 12 months | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland statutory authority to recruit experienced Principal Data Analyst. In this role, you will be responsible for providing high level analysis, reporting and advice to support management decision making on policy, programs and performance.

Key responsibilities:

Provide high level financial and operational modelling for business initiatives, programmes and projects including preparation of costings and forecasting models.

Work in a specialised environment, characterised by a regular requirement to modify and adapt established systems to deliver optimal outcomes; analyse a variety of options before choices can be made; and resolve problems within established data management systems.

Provide strategic advice, operational performance reporting, and analysis to support management to develop policy and programs.

Lead the development of statistical reports, including reports on clients, services and suppliers, employee performance targets, to improve planning and inform management decision-making.

You will also lead the preparation of statistical reports for external stakeholders as needed.

Provide high quality advice, guidance and reporting relating to individual and team income targets and time recording compliance.

Provide advice to senior management regarding the optimal configuration of business systems to support a wide range of reporting requirements.

What we’re looking for?

Proven ability to interpret complex data, identify core trends/variances, and draw accurate conclusions to support business objectives.

Deep knowledge of financial and budget management (Cash and Accrual) and a strong grasp of activity-based and unit costing.

Advanced skills in SQL and the Microsoft Suite, with high-level expertise in Excel and Fabric/Power BI.

Ideally, you have experience with Finance1, Aurion, Visualfiles, and the SQL Server stack (SSIS, SSAS, SSRS), along with SharePoint.

Ability to present clear, concise, and articulate rationale to senior management and negotiate persuasively.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Apply now or contact  Daniel Keyte on 0433 013 200 or daniel@limejobs.com.au to find out more!

A leading Consultancy partenered with a Pharmaceutical/ Life Sciences business based in the Sydney CBD is embarking on a major greenfield SAP S/4HANA transformation, and we are seeking an experienced SAP SME & Senior Finance Consultant to join the program at its inception.
This is a unique opportunity to influence the strategic direction of a multi‑year S/4HANA rollout across Finance, HR, Procurement, Supply Chain, and Manufacturing.

This initial 6‑month contract is focused exclusively on discovery, with a strong likelihood of extension as the program progresses into design and delivery.

You will be required in the client office 2 days per week in the Sydney CBD 3 days from home

The Role:
1. SAP S/4Hana SME: Purely consulting with high level stakeholders on the direction of the program ahd reporting to the Functional Lead & Solution Architect

2. SAP S/4Hana Senior Finance Consultant: Advising high level stakeholders on the blueprinting and early phase discovery for finance processes and treasury/tax

SAP S/4HANA SME

Lead and facilitate discovery workshops with C‑suite and GM‑level stakeholders
Provide expert guidance across multiple functional domains
Shape the future‑state process architecture for a greenfield environment
Support the development of scope, roadmap, and program governance
Act as a trusted advisor to senior leadership throughout the discovery phase
Deep expertise as a SAP SME within S/4HANA environments
Strong experience in discovery, blueprinting, or early‑phase program definition
Broad functional understanding across Finance, HR, Procurement, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing

SAP S/4HANA Senior Finance Consultant

Lead Finance‑focused discovery workshops with C-suite and GM‑level stakeholders
Analyse current‑state processes and define future‑state Finance requirements
Provide expertise across core Finance areas (Record to Report, Procure to Pay, Order to Cash, Asset Accounting, Controlling)
Support the development of scope, roadmap, and Finance architecture for the greenfield program
Broad understanding of Finance integration points across Procurement, Supply Chain, and Manufacturing
Experience in regulated industries (Pharma/Life Sciences advantageous)
Tax and Treasury experience highly advantageous

Apply Now

Company: Cognizant Technology Solutions Australia Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) is a leading provider of information technology, consulting, and business process services, dedicated to helping the world's leading companies build stronger businesses. Headquartered in Teaneck, New Jersey (U.S.), Cognizant combines a passion for client satisfaction, technology innovation, deep industry and business process expertise, and a global, collaborative workforce that embodies the future of work. Cognizant is a member of the NASDAQ-100, the S&P 500, the Forbes Global 2000, and the Fortune 500 and is ranked among the top performing and fastest growing companies in the world.

Our Culture

Your passion, integrity and experience are integral to Cognizant's success. You will join a dynamic and expanding global leader in IT and Business consultancy where you will be valued for who you are. We take pride in our partnership with our clients, so your ability to add value and provide exceptional service to our clients is fundamental to your success. In return, you will be presented with opportunities to develop your career and collaborate with talented colleagues in a supportive, diverse environment.

At Cognizant we recognize that companies that are open and welcoming to a multi-culturally diverse workforce will thrive with fresh perspectives and collaborative knowledge. Cognizant focuses on promoting & increasing gender diversity and providing a workplace which encourages great participation and an equal playing field, where merit and accomplishment are the only criteria for success.

Job Summary:

We are seeking an experienced Data Architect to design, build, and govern scalable, high‑performance data platforms. The role will focus on modern cloud-based architectures using Azure, Snowflake, and dbt, with strong emphasis on data modeling (Kimball), data governance, and close client engagement. The ideal candidate will bridge business requirements and technical solutions while ensuring data quality, security, and scalability.

Roles & Responsibilities:

Design and own end-to-end data architecture for enterprise analytics and reporting platforms

Lead architecture design and implementation using Azure data services (e.g., Azure Data Factory, ADLS, Synapse, Azure SQL)

Architect and optimize Snowflake data warehouses for performance, scalability, and cost-efficiency

Define and implement ELT frameworks using dbt, including testing, documentation, and lineage

Develop and review complex SQL and Python code for data transformations, performance tuning, and automation

Design dimensional data models using Kimball methodology (fact & dimension tables)

Establish data standards, best practices, and governance principles (naming, versioning, security, access control)

Collaborate with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data solutions

Act as a client-facing architect, leading solution discussions, design walkthroughs, and architecture reviews

Mentor data engineers and analytics teams on architecture patterns and implementation standards

Support initiatives related to data quality, metadata management, lineage, and observability

Required Skills & Experience:

Strong experience with Azure cloud data ecosystem

Hands-on expertise in Snowflake architecture and optimization

Strong working knowledge of dbt (core concepts, models, tests, macros)

Advanced SQL skills for analytics and data warehousing

Proficiency in Python for data processing and automation

Strong experience in dimensional data modeling (Kimball)

Enterprise data warehouse and lakehouse architecture

Batch and near‑real‑time data processing concepts

Data security, privacy, and access control

Performance tuning and cost optimization strategies

Data governance, metadata, and lineage concepts

Proven experience in client handling and stakeholder communication

Ability to present architectural designs to technical and non‑technical audiences

Experience working in consulting or multi-client environments preferred

Benefits:

Joining Cognizant will give you the opportunity to learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry, while having your finger on the pulse of emerging industry trends and working on the cutting edge of technology in your field of expertise.

We recognize that our people perform at their best when they feel valued as significant contributors and that is why at Cognizant, taking care of our employees is a priority:

·   You can pursue innovative career tracks and opportunities here

·   You can enhance your professional development through education and dedicated training

·   We’ll give you the skills you need to keep pace with the changing workplace while our compensation, benefits and wellness packages help you stay healthy and plan for the future.

Next Steps:

Please reach out to our friendly and welcoming team today to apply and register your interest for this fulltime role. We'd love to help you get your next role and enable you to fulfil your professional ambitions.

Research Analyst Internship - Full Time Position (Monday-Friday)

About KOSEC:

KOSEC – Kodari Securities, is one of Australia’s most accomplished financial institutions, has established a legacy of expertise, strategic insights, and ground-breaking research. At the heart of KOSEC lies our mission: to empower investors with unparalleled knowledge, state-of-the-art tools, and abundant resources. Committed to optimal avenues of capital preservation and wealth cultivation, KOSEC serves as a beacon for ultra-high net worth investors, corporations, trusts, SMSFs, family offices, and charities.

Program Overview:

A rare 3-month full time internship opportunity awaits at KOSEC, tailored for individuals keen to immerse themselves in the world of equity markets. 

Why KOSEC Internship?

Comprehensive Training: Delve deep into sectors and companies, master the art of daily technical analysis, and gain a comprehensive perspective of the equity market's dynamism.

State-of-the-Art Tools: Acquaint yourself with Phantom X, a ground-breaking in-house software, and redefine your analytical skill set.

Guided by the Elite: Receive guidance from industry pioneers, notably KOSEC’s visionary CEO, Michael Kodari.

Core Values at KOSEC:

Excellence: Every endeavour at KOSEC aims to surpass client expectations, setting new benchmarks in products and services.

Client-Focused Approach: Each move is calibrated towards client satisfaction, feedback, and a vision for continuous improvement.

Integrity & Accountability: At KOSEC, promises transition into unwavering commitments, and responsibilities are shouldered to bring about tangible results.

Innovation: Disruption is in the institution's DNA, constantly ushering in pioneering ideas that raise industry standards.

Transparency: Ensuring that every decision is made with utmost clarity, stakeholders are always in the loop and empowered.

Respect: Central to KOSEC's philosophy, it forges a nourishing and collaborative work environment.

Introducing Your Mentor, Michael Kodari:

Michael Kodari is widely recognized as a top-performing investor, philanthropist, and leading expert in financial markets on an international scale.

Globally acclaimed as an investment luminary, Michael consistently delivers exceptional performance, setting himself apart on the world stage. Beyond his philanthropic endeavours, he is acknowledged as one of the foremost authorities in the financial markets, earning the distinction of being hailed by CNBC Asia as 'the brightest 21st-century entrepreneur in wealth management.'

With a solid foundation in funds management and stockbroking, Michael's journey is characterised by collaborations with some of the industry's most successful minds and consultations with leading financial institutions. A trailblazer from the outset, he became the youngest expert panellist on the Sky Business Channel at a mere 25 years old, captivating audiences and achieving record-high ratings. Over three transformative years, Michael solidified his reputation as an unwavering force in the field.

His influence extends beyond the financial sector to television screens and print media, where he assumes the role of 'Money Expert' on Channel 7's weekly Sunday program 'Elevator Pitch' and contributes insights to key financial publications. With a magnetic presence, he engages in interviews across diverse media platforms and spearheads enlightening global conferences.

Michael's impact reaches the highest echelons, earning endorsements from governments worldwide. His distinguished participation in the landmark event 'Inside China’s Future,' hosted in Australia and honoured by the Chinese government, underscores his prowess in fund management. Standing alongside George Soros, he was the sole financier invited to offer invaluable insights on Chinese government asset allocation offshore, a testament to his extraordinary expertise.

In 2019, Michael's reputation was further amplified as he took centre stage as an esteemed expert panellist at Parliament House during the Australia-China Innovation & Entrepreneurship Forum, meticulously orchestrated by AUSTRAC in collaboration with the Chinese government. His profound insights and expertise significantly contributed to discussions, shedding light on the dynamic landscape of innovation and entrepreneurship between the two nations. This participation solidified his role as a trusted authority in the field of investment and collaboration on an international scale.

Your Future with KOSEC:

· Engage with diverse clientele, including individual investors, family offices, trusts, SMSFs, corporations, and charities.

· Address real-world financial challenges, providing a robust practical learning environment.

· Network with industry leaders and benefit from unparalleled mentorship opportunities.

· Gain exposure to transformative financial methodologies and practices.

Job Title: Data Analyst - Data Migration and Reporting
Ad Text:
Catholic Care Broken Bay (CCDBB), delivers a wide range of Social Services through its workforce of ~1000 staff. With the need for Social Services in Australia ever increasing, CCDBB has embarked on an ambitious multi-year enterprise wide Transformation, to lay the foundations for future growth, improve Client Care, improve staff working conditions, improve the ability to manage Risk and Compliance, and also launch new Services. As a result, this Transformation is in need of a Data Analyst, with a focus on Data Migration and Reporting.

The successful candidate will be a critical enabler of the CCDBB’s success, as part of a highly committed Transformation and Technology Team, and will get an opportunity to work across all lines of business, across an extremely diverse group of Social Services Programs, with exposure to SLT members and Senior Management. Working in the Not For Profit sector, the successful candidate will also feel the deep meaning of knowing their work contributes to helping CCDBB’s Clients who need care.

About the role
The Data Analyst – Data Migration and Reporting, is responsible for ensuring high‑quality, reliable, and strategically valuable data within the organisation’s Salesforce environment. This role has a strong focus on data migration from legacy systems, data modelling, and the development of insightful dashboards and reports using both Salesforce and Power BI. Working closely with Project Managers, Program Managers, technical teams, and business stakeholders, the Data Analys – Data Migration and Reporting plays a critical role in supporting successful system implementation, organisational reporting, and evidence‑based decision‑making.
  
Key Responsibilities:

Data Migration & Data Quality
Lead and execute end‑to‑end data migration activities from legacy systems into Salesforce.
Map, transform, cleanse, and validate data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency.
Develop and maintain data dictionaries, migration templates, and data quality rules.
Conduct data audits and reconciliation to ensure migrated data meets business and compliance requirements.
Collaborate with technical teams to troubleshoot data issues and optimise migration processes.
Reporting, Dashboards & Data Visualisation
Design, build, and maintain Salesforce dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Develop Power BI dashboards that integrate Salesforce and other organisational datasets.
Translate complex datasets into clear, meaningful visual insights.
Ensure reporting outputs align with organisational KPIs and program outcomes.
Business Analysis & Stakeholder Engagement
Work with business units to understand data needs and reporting requirements.
Facilitate workshops, requirements gathering sessions, and data validation activities.
Provide subject matter expertise on Salesforce data structures and reporting capabilities.
Project & Program Management Alignment
Contribute to project planning, risk management, and milestone tracking.
Prepare documentation including data migration plans, test scripts, and progress reports.
Support Program Managers and Project Managers with data insights and impact assessments.
Ensure all data‑related activities align with project governance and quality standards.
Continuous Improvement & Data Governance
Identify opportunities to streamline data processes and improve data integrity.
Support the development of data governance frameworks and standards.
Provide training and guidance to staff on data literacy and reporting tools.
  
Key Capabilities & Skills:

Technical Skills

Proficiency in Salesforce (Reports & Dashboards, Data Loader, Data Import Wizard).
Demonstrated experience in data migration and data cleansing.
Advanced Power BI skills (data modelling, DAX, visualisation best practice).
Experience with ETL/ELT concepts and tools, consuming relational databases, and REST/SOAP API’s required.
Experience with Microsoft Fabric, or other Lakehouse platforms (Snowflake, Databricks) desired but not necessary.
Understanding of relational databases and data governance principles.
Analytical & Problem‑Solving Skills

Ability to interpret complex datasets and identify patterns, risks, and insights.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
Ability to troubleshoot data issues and propose practical solutions.
Project & Stakeholder Skills
Experience working within project or program management environments.
Ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical information.
Collaborative working style with the ability to influence stakeholders.
Qualifications & Experience

Tertiary qualification in Information Systems, Data Analytics, Business, or related field.
Experience in Salesforce data analysis, data migration, or CRM analytics.
Experience developing dashboards in Salesforce and Power BI.
Experience in structured project environments (Agile, PRINCE2, PMBOK).
Salesforce certifications (Administrator, Data Architect, Business Analyst) highly regarded.
Power BI certification desirable.
Experience in the Not For Profit sector desirable.
Key Outcomes for the Role

Successful migration of accurate, high‑quality data from legacy systems into Salesforce.
Reliable dashboards and reports that support decision‑making.
Strong alignment with project and program management disciplines.
Improved organisational data literacy and confidence in Salesforce.
Enhanced data governance, data quality, and reporting consistency.
Why join CatholicCare Broken Bay?

This is an opportunity to contribute to a significant transformation program within a purpose-driven organisation. You will work across a diverse range of services, gain exposure to senior leadership, and play a key role in strengthening data capability to support better outcomes for the community.
In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, flexibility and lifestyle:

Salary Packaging Benefits
Increase your take-home pay through not-for-profit salary packaging options, allowing you to pay for everyday expenses before tax.
Flexible Work Options (WFH)
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance with flexible working arrangements
Fitness Passport
Access a wide network of gyms, pools and fitness facilities at a discounted rate to support your health and wellbeing.
Additional Leave Benefits
Benefit from initiatives such as CatholicCare Day and other leave options that support rest, wellbeing and personal commitments.
Supportive & Collaborative Culture
Be part of a values-driven organisation where collaboration, respect and continuous improvement are at the core of how we work.
Professional Development Opportunities
Grow your career with access to learning, development and exposure to large-scale organisational transformation initiatives.
APPLY NOW

If you are looking to apply your data expertise in a role that delivers meaningful impact, we encourage you to apply.

About Us 
Automic Group brings together modern purpose-built technology with a dynamic and experienced team to tailor solutions for companies and funds to understand, engage and manage their investors. We provide a cloud-native single registry, employee share plan and board management platform, and professional services including company secretarial, ESG, accounting and finance, and investor and media relations. 

Today, over 1,400 ASX listed and unlisted clients trust Automic’s 250 employees to manage their registry, investor and board requirements – and Automic is ranked number one on the ASX by number of companies serviced, with 750 listed clients. In the past two financial years, Automic has won 50%of IPOs and 70% of listed companies who have switched registries. 

Our culture is innately entrepreneurial, comprising people who thrive in an atmosphere where ideas, actions and initiative are encouraged. Our core values, Customer Success, Ambition, Equality and Team are at the foundation of who we are and how we operate.

We are growing and are looking for an experienced Senior Technical Business Analyst to join our team in Sydney!


About the Role

This role requires an experienced BA who can independently own complex system changes end-to-end and who can work closely with business and technical stakeholders to deliver build-ready requirements as our tech grows.

Key Responsibilities include:
Operating in strict accordance with Automic’s Information Security Management System (ISMS), ensuring compliance with security, governance, and data protection standards.
Leading end-to-end business analysis activities across new features, enhancements, and strategic initiatives.
Collaborating with senior management, IT, Sales, Marketing, and Operations to gather, analyse, and prioritise business requirements.
Facilitating and driving requirements workshops, solution design sessions, and stakeholder discussions.
Working closely with developers and UX designers to refine and clarify requirements throughout the delivery lifecycle.
Translating high-value business priorities into clearly defined Minimum Viable Products (MVPs).
Supporting testing and validation activities to ensure delivered solutions meet business intent.
Identifying risks, dependencies, and impacts across systems and processes.
Continuously improving analysis practices, documentation standards, and tools to enhance productivity, quality, and security.

About you!

 

To be successful in this role, we are looking for someone with:
A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Finance, or related discipline.
At least 8+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst in enterprise software environments working within an Agile delivery framework
Demonstrated experience delivering across full software development life cycles.
Proven hands-on technical capability from functional requirements gathering to user stories & high-quality acceptance criteria & mapping multisystem impact analysis
Able to work independently owning delivery outcomes, escalating risk when required
Proven experience of leading structured workshops with key stakeholders able to manage up effectively to get the right outcomes
A systems level thinker, understanding cross system impacts
Experience within financial services is highly desirable, particularly within share/unit registry, managed funds, fund admin, wealth/ market infrastructure or investor lifecycle platforms
Why join Automic:

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment.

Key Benefits include:

Hybrid working
Professional career growth & development
Supportive & fast paced environment
Opportunity to grow with the business

If the above sounds like you, please apply for this exciting opportunity and be part of the growth journey with Automic!
